faqs.q: What should I do if I'm having suicidal thoughts?
faqs.a: You are not alone and help is available right now. In the United States, you can call or text 988 to reach the Suicide and Crisis Lifeline any time, day or night, and if you or someone else is in immediate danger, call 911.

faqs.q: Does having suicidal thoughts mean I will act on them?
faqs.a: No. Suicidal ideation isn't a guarantee of action, and it isn't permanent. The thoughts often reflect overwhelming pain rather than what a person truly wants, and many people who have lived through intense suicidal thoughts go on to feel very differently once they get support.

faqs.q: Does talking about suicide make it worse?
faqs.a: No. Talking about suicide with a caring person does not plant the idea or make things worse, and saying the thoughts out loud to someone safe often brings relief. It opens a door to help, and effective treatments exist. In the United States you can call or text 988 any time.
